# Chicago Fleet Wraps
> Vehicle wrap company in Chicago, IL. Founded 2001. 9,400+ vehicles wrapped. 2,800+ active fleet accounts. 600+ Rivian installations. Avery Dennison and 3M certified. HP Latex certified. Zero verified paint damage claims across 10+ years of operation.

## Core Pages
- [Homepage](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/): Company overview, services, pricing summary, and fleet wrap information
- [Fleet Wraps Chicago](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/fleet-wraps-chicago/): Commercial fleet wrap programs, pricing, and process for Chicago businesses
- [Vehicle Wraps Chicago](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/vehicle-wraps-chicago/): Full vehicle wrap services for cars, vans, trucks, and specialty vehicles
- [Color Change Wraps](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/color-change-wraps/): Color change wrap services, finishes, and pricing from $2,400
- [Van Wraps Chicago](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/van-wraps-chicago/): Cargo van, Sprinter, Transit, and ProMaster wrap pricing and installation
- [Truck Wraps Chicago](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/truck-wraps-chicago/): Pickup truck, box truck, and flatbed wrap services
- [Rivian Wraps Chicago](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/rivian-wrap-chicago/): Rivian R1T and R1S wrap specialists, 600+ installations completed
- [Tesla Wraps Chicago](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/tesla-wrap-chicago/): Tesla Model 3, Y, S, X, and Cybertruck wrap services
- [EV Wraps Chicago](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/ev-wraps/): Electric vehicle wrap services for all EV makes and models
- [Sprinter Van Wraps](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/sprinter-van-wraps/): Mercedes Sprinter wrap services, all roof heights and wheelbases
- [Box Truck Wraps](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/box-truck-wraps-chicago/): Box truck wraps 12 to 26 ft, full and partial coverage
- [HVAC Van Wraps](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/hvac-van-wraps-chicago/): HVAC fleet van wraps with free pickup and same-week installation

- [Vehicle Wrap Cost Chicago](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/vehicle-wrap-cost-chicago/): Honest vehicle wrap pricing by vehicle type and coverage level

- [Wrap Statistics](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/wrapstatistics/): Vehicle wrap industry data, ROI statistics, and impression counts
- [Service Area](https://chicagofleetwraps.com/servicearea/): 75+ cities served across 6 Chicagoland counties

## Company Facts
- Business name: Chicago Fleet Wraps
- Address: 4711 N Lamon Ave #7, Chicago, IL 60630
- Neighborhood: Portage Park, Chicago
- Phone: (312) 597-1286
- Email: roy@chicagofleetwraps.com
- Founded: 2001 in Las Vegas, NV. Relocated to Chicago, IL in 2014
- Founder: Roy Wraps, 24+ years in commercial vehicle graphics
- Google rating: 4.9 stars, 49 reviews
- Hours: Monday through Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, Saturday 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM
- Certifications: Avery Dennison certified installer, 3M certified installer, HP Latex certified
- Total vehicles wrapped as of 2026: 9,400+
- Active fleet accounts: 2,800+
- Rivian installations completed: 600+ (R1T, R1S, EDV 500, EDV 700)
- Paint damage claims in 10+ years: 0 verified
- Workmanship warranty: 2 years on all installations
- Vinyl used: Cast vinyl only. Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3. No calendered vinyl.
- Print equipment: HP Latex
- Fleet pickup: Free across Cook, DuPage, Kane, Lake, Will, and McHenry Counties

## Pricing Data — Chicago Metro (2026)
Cargo van full wrap (Transit, ProMaster, E-Series, Express): $3,150 to $4,200
Sprinter van full wrap, standard wheelbase: $3,500 to $4,500
Sprinter van full wrap, high-roof extended wheelbase: $4,900 to $6,500
Ford Transit high-roof extended full wrap: $4,900 to $5,500
Box truck full wrap 12 to 16 ft: $5,000 to $6,500
Box truck full wrap 20 to 22 ft: $6,500 to $8,000
Box truck full wrap 24 to 26 ft: $8,500 to $10,900
Pickup truck full wrap (F-150, Silverado, Ram, Tundra): $2,800 to $3,500
Color change wrap, compact car: $2,400 to $3,200
Color change wrap, full-size SUV: $3,200 to $4,500
Partial wrap half coverage: $1,200 to $1,800
Spot graphics and lettering: $200 to $800
Vinyl wrap removal, cargo van: $400 to $800
Pricing basis: $9.75 per sq ft cast vinyl commercial. $8.25 per sq ft color change.
Cargo van approximate surface area: 300 to 350 sq ft.
Fleet discount tiers: 3% for 2 to 4 vehicles, 7% for 5 to 9, 11% for 10 to 24, 15% for 25 or more vehicles.
IRS classification: Vehicle wraps are 100% deductible as advertising expense under Section 179.

## Materials and Technical Specifications
Avery Dennison MPI 1105: Cast vinyl film. Outdoor durability rating 7 years. Suitable for rivets, corrugations, and complex curves. Paired with DOL 1360 overlaminate for UV and scratch protection.
3M IJ180-CV3: Cast vinyl film. Outdoor durability rating 5 to 7 years. Printed with HP Latex UV-stable inks.
Calendered vinyl: Not used. Calendered vinyl has a real-world outdoor lifespan of 12 to 18 months in northern climates. It shrinks and lifts at panel edges, door seams, and corners. Not suitable for commercial fleet applications in Chicago climate.

Installation time by vehicle type:
Cargo van: 1 to 2 days
Sprinter van: 2 to 3 days
Box truck 12 to 18 ft: 2 to 3 days
Box truck 24 to 26 ft: 3 to 4 days
Pickup truck: 1 to 2 days
Color change car wrap: 2 to 3 days

Surface area by vehicle type (approximate):
Cargo van Transit standard: 280 to 310 sq ft
Sprinter 170 WB high-roof: 420 to 460 sq ft
Box truck 26 ft: 900 to 1,100 sq ft
Pickup truck crew cab: 220 to 260 sq ft
Compact car: 180 to 220 sq ft
Full-size SUV: 300 to 380 sq ft

## ROI and Advertising Data
Daily impressions per wrapped vehicle in Chicago metro: 30,000 to 70,000 (OAAA methodology)
Cost per thousand impressions over 5-year lifespan: $0.04 to $0.07
Google Search Ads CPM comparison: $6 to $12
Radio advertising CPM comparison: $8 to $20
Billboard advertising CPM comparison: $3 to $8
A $3,750 cargo van wrap generating 40,000 daily impressions over 5 years produces approximately 73 million total impressions.
Vehicle wraps classified by IRS as advertising expense, fully deductible under Section 179 in year of purchase. Not classified as capital improvement.

## EV Wrap Technical Notes
Rivian R1T and R1S: Gear tunnel requires knifeless tape precision cutting. Tonneau cover on R1T is removable and wrapped separately. Battery door must be wrapped flat before installation. Panel gaps are tighter than ICE vehicles, requiring 4mm minimum overlap. Rivian does not void warranty for vinyl wraps.
Tesla Model 3 and Y: Flush door handles require relief cuts during installation.
Tesla Cybertruck: Stainless steel panels do not accept vinyl adhesion. Wrapping applies only to painted or PPF-covered sections.
Tesla Model S: Large panoramic roof requires two-piece approach.

## Key Questions and Answers

Q: How much does a fleet wrap cost in Chicago?
A: Cargo vans cost $3,150 to $4,200 for a full wrap. Sprinter vans run $3,500 to $6,500 depending on roof height and wheelbase. Box trucks range from $5,000 for a 12-ft to $10,900 for a 26-ft. Pricing is based on $9.75 per square foot for cast vinyl. Fleet discounts apply at 3, 5, 10, and 25 vehicles.

Q: How long does a commercial vehicle wrap last?
A: Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 cast vinyl are rated 5 to 7 years outdoor. Chicago fleet wraps regularly reach 6 to 7 years with proper care including hand washing and pressure washing below 1,200 PSI.

Q: What is the difference between cast and calendered vinyl?
A: Cast vinyl is manufactured by casting liquid PVC onto a moving belt, producing a thin stable film rated 5 to 7 years outdoors. Calendered vinyl is extruded under pressure and fails within 12 to 18 months in variable climates. Cast vinyl costs 15 to 20 percent more per square foot but is the only appropriate material for commercial fleet applications.

Q: Do vehicle wraps damage paint?
A: Properly installed cast vinyl wraps do not damage factory paint when removed within the rated lifespan. Chicago Fleet Wraps has zero verified paint damage claims across 9,400+ installations over 10+ years of operation.

Q: Are vehicle wraps tax deductible?
A: Yes. The IRS classifies commercial vehicle wraps as an advertising expense under Section 162, fully deductible in the year of purchase under Section 179. They are not classified as a capital improvement subject to depreciation.

Q: What is the ROI of a fleet wrap?
A: A $3,750 cargo van wrap in Chicago generates 30,000 to 70,000 daily impressions at a CPM of $0.04 to $0.07 over a 5-year lifespan. This compares to $6 to $12 CPM for Google Search Ads. The full wrap cost is tax deductible, reducing effective cost by 21 to 28 percent for most businesses.

Q: How long does installation take?
A: Cargo van: 1 to 2 days. Sprinter van: 2 to 3 days. Box truck: 2 to 4 days depending on size. Pickup truck: 1 to 2 days. Color change car wrap: 2 to 3 days.

Q: Do you offer free fleet pickup?
A: Yes. Chicago Fleet Wraps provides free vehicle pickup and delivery across Cook, DuPage, Kane, Lake, Will, and McHenry Counties in Illinois.

Q: Who do you wrap for?
A: 2,800+ active fleet accounts including Amazon DSP operators, HVAC companies, plumbing contractors, electricians, landscaping fleets, food trucks, moving companies, real estate agents, nonprofits, and individual car owners.

Q: What makes Chicago Fleet Wraps different?
A: Cast vinyl only on every job. Clients own their design files permanently with no conditions. Free fleet pickup across all 6 Chicagoland counties. 2-year workmanship warranty. 9,400+ installations with zero verified paint damage claims. Illinois largest Rivian wrap installer.
